{{Quote|According to an Imperial spy, Zaarin and his senior staff know about the truth about the Emperor's plans for the Rebels at Endor. One of Zaarin's top commanders, Namuura Din, upon discovering this information, has decided to defect to the Rebel side.|An [[Imperial briefing officer]]|audio=BriefingOfficer-DinRebels-TIEFighter.flac|<ref name="TIE Fighter CD-ROM" />}}
'''Namuura Din''' was a [[Commander/Legends|commander]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], serving as one of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Demetrius Zaarin]]'s top commanders and later joining him when he [[Zaarin insurrection|splintered]] off from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] and founded a [[Zaarin coup forces|new faction]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Upon learning of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Battle of Endor/Legends|plans]] for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] and the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon's]] fully operational status, Din decided to defect to the Alliance and provide them with the information and a [[Sealion|Missile Boat]]. While he met with the Rebel [[MC80 Star Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] ''[[Link]]'' at a rendezvous point aboard his [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]], the ''[[Zeplin]]'', and successfully transferred them, Imperial forces attacked the [[Starship/Legends|ships]], and the ''Zeplin'' was captured with Din aboard. He divulged his knowledge to the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] in a subsequent interrogation.

==Biography==
===Defector===
{{Quote|Namuura Din was one of Zaarin's top commanders. He may know the true status of the new Death Star.|An Imperial briefing [[Officer/Legends|officer]]|audio=BriefingOfficer-DinStatus-TIEFighter.flac|<ref name="TIE Fighter CD-ROM" />}}
[[File:Zeplin-TF.png|thumb|right|300px|The ''Zeplin'', Namuura Din's command ship]]
Namuura Din held the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Commander/Legends|commander]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]]. With the [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Zeplin]]'' as his [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]], he was one of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Demetrius Zaarin]]'s top commanders. Amid the ongoing [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the Empire and [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], Din joined Zaarin when the admiral [[Zaarin insurrection|betrayed]] the Empire and formed his [[Zaarin coup forces|own faction]] to strike against them.<ref name="TIE Fighter CD-ROM" />

According to an Imperial [[Spy/Legends|spy]], Zaarin and his senior staff were aware of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Battle of Endor/Legends|plans]] for the Alliance at<ref name="TIE Fighter CD-ROM" /> the [[Moon/Legends|moon]]<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] and the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] being fully operational. Having learned of this from Zaarin, Din decided to betray him and defect to the Alliance in order to pass the information on to them as well as supply them with [[Sealion|one]] of the few [[Missile Boat]]s the admiral had under his possession.<ref name="TIE Fighter CD-ROM" /> In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="TIE Fighter Date">{{TIEFighterDate}}</ref> Din was aboard the ''Zeplin'' when it entered deep within the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] home area near<ref name="TIE Fighter Collector Guide" /> the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="NEC" /> [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in the midst of a [[Second battle of Imperial Center|battle]] between Zaarin's [[Zaarin's fleet|forces]] and the Empire. The ''Zeplin'' stayed for a short while before jumping into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]].<ref name="TIE Fighter Collector Guide" />

===Capture===
{{Quote|Lord Vader, in his usual efficient manner, has extracted important information from Din.|An Imperial briefing officer, following Din's capture|audio=BriefingOfficer-DinInfo-TIEFighter.flac|<ref name="TIE Fighter CD-ROM" />}}
{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: TIE Fighter Collector's CD-ROM}}
Following the battle, the ''Zeplin'' met with the Rebel [[MC80 Star Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] ''[[Link]]'' at a rendezvous point so that Din could pass the information on to the Rebel [[Commanding officer/Legends|commander]] and hand over the Missile Boat. Although the operation was a success, the ''Zeplin'' was [[Capture of the Zeplin|intercepted]] by [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]] consisting of [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|Assault Gunboats]] and the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Garrett (Star Destroyer)|''Garrett'']]. The ''Link'' escaped shortly after with the Missile Boat and information intact. As the [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]] retaliated, it was disabled by the [[Gunboat/Legends|gunboats']] [[NK-3 ion cannon|ion cannons]] and boarded by [[Delta-class DX-9 stormtrooper transport|transports]] launched from the ''Garrett'', resulting in both the ship and Din's capture.<ref name="TIE Fighter CD-ROM" />

In the battle's aftermath, the ''Zeplin'' was sent to an Imperial [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]] for an examination by [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] while Vader interrogated Din, who confessed both what he had learned from Zaarin and what the Rebels had received to the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]]. Subsequently, the Empire [[Capture of the Link|prevented]] the ''Link'' from reaching [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] and [[Recovery of the Sealion|recovered]] the stolen Missile Boat.<ref name="TIE Fighter CD-ROM" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
Namuura Din was created for "Enemies of the Empire," an expansion pack for [[Totally Games]]' [[1994]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']], which was included in the game's [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter Collector's CD-ROM|''Collector's CD-ROM'']] in [[1995]].<ref name="TIE Fighter CD-ROM">[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter Collector's CD-ROM|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'' Collector's CD-ROM]]</ref> Din was also mentioned in [[Prima Games]]' [[1996]] [[TIE Fighter Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] for the game. In one instance, the guide misspells the character's first name as "Namurra." The guide also states that Din was suspected by the Empire to have received information on the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] by the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]],<ref name="TIE Fighter Collector Guide">''[[TIE Fighter Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide]]''</ref> a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Species/Legends|species]],<ref name="Bothan CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|88|Bothan}}</ref> contradicting ''TIE Fighter'' previously stating he had learned the information from Zaarin. This article follows the game's version of events.<ref name="TIE Fighter CD-ROM" />
